secondaryActions.label: Save and review
secondaryActions.evidence: page-objects/notification/CountryOfOriginPage.ts:13-15 — btnSaveAndReview exposes a 'Save and review' button variant on the country-of-origin/origin page (hub re-entry / review flow). Not rendered in the create-journey trace, which showed only 'Save and continue'.

secondaryActions.label: Save and return to hub
secondaryActions.evidence: page-objects/notification/CountryOfOriginPage.ts:17-19 — btnSaveAndReturnToHub exposes a 'Save and return to hub' button variant (hub-driven amend flow). Not rendered in the create-journey trace.

structure.text: About the consignment
Origin of the import
Country of origin
Select a country / Afghanistan / … (254 options, reference data)
Does the consignment require a region of origin code?
This is only required if the consignment needs a health certificate that shows a region of origin code.
Yes / No (No checked by default)
Enter the region code
structure.govukComponent: Label (govuk-label--m) — inside conditional reveal for Yes

structure.text: Enter up to 5 characters. (verbatim copy; note the input actually enforces maxlength=3 — copy/attribute mismatch)
region-code (conditional text input, width-3, maxlength=3)
Country from where consigned
Select country from where consigned / Afghanistan / … (254 options, reference data)
Does this consignment conform to regulatory requirements?
This refers to points (a) and (d) of Assimilated Regulation 2017/625.
Yes / No (each with a per-option hint; Yes checked by default)
Will the consignment change vehicles or means of transport after the Border Control Post (BCP)?
Yes / No (No checked by default)
Add a reference number for this consignment (optional)
This can be whatever internal reference you use for the consignment.
local-reference-number (text input)
Save and continue

govukComponents.usage: Section caption 'About the consignment' above the H1
Country of origin and Country from where consigned dropdowns (254 options each — reference data)
Three Yes/No radio groups: region-of-origin (conditional reveal), conform-to-regulations (with per-item hints), and change-of-transport-after-BCP
Wraps each of the three radio groups with an --m legend
Conditional 'Enter the region code' input (width-3) and 'Add a reference number' input
Six govuk-hint elements: region-of-origin group hint, region-code field hint, conform-to-regulations group hint, two per-item radios__hint (conform Yes + conform No), and local reference number hint
Field labels (--m) and radio option labels (__label)
'points (a) and (d) of Assimilated Regulation 2017/625.' link inside the conform-to-regulations hint (to legislation.gov.uk)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom classes on the conditional 'Enter the region code' reveal that appears when 'Does the consignment require a region of origin code?' = Yes. region-code wraps the conditional block, region-code-box wraps the input, and region-code-divider is a visual separator element.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke IPAFFS styling layered on top of the standard govuk-radios--conditional reveal. Not part of the Design System — the divider in particular is decoration the toolbox does not ship.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: The reveal itself is already a standard Radios conditional (govuk-radios--conditional / govuk-radios__conditional). The new app can drop the custom box/divider classes and use the plain conditional reveal with a govuk-input for the code.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The name/id 'conform-uk-regulations' also appears as a CSS class on the conform-to-regulations markup — an IPAFFS-specific selector hook rather than a Design System class.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Field-name-as-class is a bespoke hook, likely for JS or targeted styling; it is not a govuk-* utility.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: No replacement needed — the group is a standard Radios with per-item govuk-radios__hint. Drop the custom class in the rebuild.

fields.name: origin-country
fields.label: Country of origin
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 15 — select name=origin-country, 254 options incl. placeholder 'Select a country'. Reference data (countries); new app should source from a reference-data service, not hardcode. Note list includes England/Scotland/Wales/Northern Ireland as separate entries. Corroboration (inferred): the QA suite drives this via a SEPARATE 'Country of Origin' page — page-objects/notification/CountryOfOriginPage.ts:6-8 getByLabel('Country of origin'). Config defaults: EU euCountryOfOrigin=Austria, ROW rowCountryOfOrigin=Afghanistan (workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:27-30,124-125); a second EU variant uses Spain (workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:654). The IUU (fish/catch-certificate) flow drives ROW-only rowCountryOfOrigin=Afghanistan (workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:823-824,858). Additional observed value 'India' from the amend/manipulation test (tests/notification/ched-p/ched-p-manipulation.spec.ts:44,67); that test also asserts the amended value renders on the review page (countryOfOriginValue toHaveText 'India', :67) and that amending country of origin CLEARS the consignment purpose downstream (:47-48). Option VALUES are ISO country codes (e.g. Afghanistan='AF', Austria='AT', Aland Islands='AX') with the country name as the visible label — see types/country.ts:1-20. The field is amendable post-creation via a 'Change country of origin' link (tests/notification/ched-p/ched-p-manipulation.spec.ts:42).

fields.name: region-code-option
fields.label: Does the consignment require a region of origin code?
fields.hint: This is only required if the consignment needs a health certificate that shows a region of origin code.
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 15 — radios name=region-code-option (ids region-code-yes / region-code-no), 'No' checked by default. govuk-radios--conditional reveals the region-code input on 'Yes'. No QA page object exposes this control — the OriginOfImportPage page object (page-objects/notification/OriginOfImportPage.ts) drives only the conform and change-after-BCP radios; every workflow accepts the default 'No' here, so the 'Yes' branch and required-when-shown behaviour are trace/inference only, never asserted by a test.

fields.name: region-code
fields.label: Enter the region code
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 15 — text input name=region-code, class 'govuk-input govuk-input--width-3', hidden via govuk-radios__conditional--hidden until 'Yes' selected (region-code-no is checked by default). CONFIRMED maxlength=3 on the input element (verified via outerHTML: maxlength="3"). NOTE — the field hint reads verbatim 'Enter up to 5 characters.' (id-less govuk-hint) which CONTRADICTS the enforced maxlength of 3; this is an IPAFFS copy/attribute inconsistency to resolve in the rebuild, not an inference. required-when-shown is a gap; no error state captured.

fields.name: consigned-country
fields.label: Country from where consigned
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 15 — select name=consigned-country, 254 options; placeholder 'Select country from where consigned' is [disabled]. Reference data (countries); source from reference-data service in new app. No QA page object or workflow drives this field — CountryOfOriginPage exposes only 'Country of origin', not 'Country from where consigned' — so its required flag is trace-only and never corroborated by a test assertion (gap: distinct-from-origin validation, if any).

fields.name: conform-uk-regulations
fields.label: Does this consignment conform to regulatory requirements?
fields.hint: This refers to points (a) and (d) of Assimilated Regulation 2017/625.

optionHints.Yes: The consignment conforms to the regulatory requirements and the goods are for the internal market, transit, transshipment or movement for ship supply.
optionHints.No: The consignment does not conform to regulatory requirements. Transits, transshipments, or movement for ship supply may not need to meet the regulatory requirements, if not intended for the internal market.

fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 15 — radios name=conform-uk-regulations (ids conform-uk-regulations / conform-uk-regulations-2), 'Yes' checked by default. The VISIBLE radio labels are exactly 'Yes' and 'No' (confirmed via label[for] textContent); the long sentences are per-item govuk-radios__hint elements (ids conform-uk-regulations-item-hint / conform-uk-regulations-2-item-hint, class 'govuk-hint govuk-radios__hint'), captured verbatim in optionHints above — they are NOT part of the option label. Group hint contains link 'points (a) and (d) of Assimilated Regulation 2017/625.' (https://www.legislation.gov.uk/eur/2017/625/article/1). Corroboration (inferred): page-objects/notification/OriginOfImportPage.ts:6-8 targets group 'Does this consignment conform' → 'Yes'; every ched-p workflow and the accessibility spec hardcode radioConformsYes.check() (workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:282,676,862; tests/accessibility/ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:36). The 'No' branch is never exercised by the suite.

fields.name: transport-details-required
fields.label: Will the consignment change vehicles or means of transport after the Border Control Post (BCP)?
fields.conditionalOn: always shown; selecting Yes drives an after-BCP transport leg later in the journey (transport-details-required=true)
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 15 — radios name=transport-details-required (ids transport-details-required / transport-details-required-2, value='true' for Yes), 'No' checked by default. This is the change-of-destination / change-of-transport-after-BCP driver; the test checks 'Yes' here. Corroboration (inferred): page-objects/notification/OriginOfImportPage.ts:13-15 radioChangeAfterBcpYes targets group 'Will the consignment change' → 'Yes'; every ched-p workflow AND the IUU fish flow hardcode radioChangeAfterBcpYes.check() (workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:283,677,863; tests/accessibility/ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:37) — so 'Yes' (the after-BCP transport leg) is the only exercised branch; the 'No' branch is never driven.

fields.name: local-reference-number
fields.label: Add a reference number for this consignment (optional)
fields.hint: This can be whatever internal reference you use for the consignment.
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 15 — text input name=local-reference-number, label suffixed '(optional)'. No QA page object drives this field; the '(optional)' suffix is the only evidence it is not required (no test asserts it), consistent with an optional free-text field.

openQuestions: No error/validation state was captured for this page in this trace (0 errors). Verbatim validation copy for a missing origin-country, consigned-country, or unselected radio group is a gap — mine an errored trace if one exercised this page.

region-code maxlength is CONFIRMED 3 (input attribute), but its hint reads 'Enter up to 5 characters.' — an in-app copy/attribute mismatch a human should resolve for the rebuild. required-when-shown remains a gap (no error state captured).
Page title '<title>Add a reference number for this consignment</title>' matches the last field, not the H1 'Origin of the import' — likely an IPAFFS title-generation quirk; flag for the rebuild.
URL pattern derived from the returnUrl in page chrome ('.../DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979/consignment/page-2'); the canonical route name for the new app is a gap.
The conform-to-regulations 'No' branch and the region-of-origin 'Yes' branch were not exercised in this trace (defaults kept), so any downstream conditional pages they open are not evidenced here.

PAGE-BOUNDARY DISCREPANCY (finding): the rendered trace (action 15) shows ONE page (page-2) carrying origin-country, region-code-option, region-code, consigned-country, conform-uk-regulations, transport-details-required and local-reference-number together. But the QA suite models these as TWO separate pages — a 'Country of Origin Page' (country dropdown + its own Save and continue) followed by an 'Origin of Import Page' (conform + change-after-BCP radios + Save and continue). Both the workflow (two sequential Save-and-continue clicks: ched-p-workflows.ts:279 then :284) and the accessibility spec (distinct checkPageAccessibility snapshots 'Country of Origin Page' at :32 and 'Origin of Import Page' at :38) treat them as distinct pages. Either the trace corpus was recorded against a newer IPAFFS build that merged the two, or the getByLabel selectors resolve across a navigation. Rendered reality (merged single page) is kept as confirmed; a human should confirm whether the NEW app should present one merged page or preserve the split — and where consigned-country / region-code / local-reference-number actually live in the split version (they have no page-object counterpart at all).

The OriginOfImportPage page object (page-objects/notification/OriginOfImportPage.ts:10-12) also exposes radioHealthCertificateNo — group 'Does your consignment require a health certificate?' → 'No'. This control is NOT on the origin-of-import page: the accessibility spec shows a SEPARATE downstream 'Health Certificate Required Page' (ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:64) reached after Select Risk Category. The page object simply groups it here for convenience. Recorded as a cross-reference so it is NOT mistaken for an origin-of-import field; it belongs to its own later page in the journey and no workflow ever drives it (dead locator in the suite).

Four of the seven fields (region-code-option, region-code, consigned-country, local-reference-number) have NO QA page-object or workflow counterpart — the automated suite never touches them and accepts the rendered defaults. Their required/optional flags and any conditional/validation behaviour rest on trace evidence alone and cannot be corroborated by a test assertion. Human confirmation needed on: is consigned-country required and must it differ from origin-country; is region-code mandatory + maxlength when region-code-option=Yes.
